guidelines.text: Abstract should be concise (typically 150-250 words) and focused on problem, method, and results.
Section flow should follow Abstract, Keywords, Introduction, Methodology, Results, Conclusion, References.
Use numeric citations in square brackets ordered by first appearance.
Figures must be numbered sequentially and captions should be placed below the figure.
Abstract should summarize objective, method, and findings in up to 250 words.
Section flow should follow Title, Abstract, Introduction, Method, Results, Discussion, References.
Use author-date citations and alphabetized reference entries with hanging indents.
Figures must include descriptive captions and be referenced in text.
Abstract should be a compact summary with key findings and practical significance.
Common order is Abstract, Keywords, Introduction, Related Work, Methodology, Results, Discussion, References.
Reference style may vary by venue; maintain consistency and complete bibliographic fields.
Figures require clear labels, readable resolution, and numbered captions.
Abstract should include motivation, approach, and key contributions succinctly.
Typical order is Abstract, CCS Concepts, Keywords, Introduction, Method, Evaluation, Conclusion, References.
Use numeric or ACM-specific bibliography style as required by venue template.
Figures must be cited in text and captions should concisely describe findings.
Abstract should highlight novelty and principal findings with strong clarity.
Typical order is Abstract, Main Text, Methods, References, Acknowledgements, Author Contributions.
Use Nature citation style with numbered references and strict formatting.
Figures must be publication quality, with concise legends and panel labels where needed.
Abstract should present context, methods, major findings, and implications.
Common order is Abstract, Keywords, Introduction, Materials and Methods, Results, Discussion, Conclusions, References.
Use the target journal citation style consistently, often numeric for engineering journals.
Provide high-resolution figures with informative captions and source data notes when applicable.
Abstract should summarize question, approach, findings, and significance in concise prose.
Section flow should follow Abstract, Introduction, Main Sections, Conclusion, References.
Use author-year Harvard referencing style and ensure complete bibliographic metadata.
Figures should be clearly numbered and cross-referenced from relevant sections.
Abstract should briefly state the topic, methods, and principal argument or findings.
Section flow should follow Introduction, Body Sections, Conclusion, and Bibliography/References.
Use Chicago footnote-bibliography or author-date style consistently as configured.
Figures should include source attribution and descriptive captions.
Abstract, when required, should summarize thesis and principal evidence.
Section flow should follow Introduction, Analysis Sections, Conclusion, and Works Cited.
Use MLA author-page in-text citations with Works Cited formatted per MLA guidance.
Figures should include figure labels and source documentation where applicable.
Abstract should summarize objective, methods, results, and conclusion clearly.
Section flow should follow Abstract, Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, References.
Use numbered references in citation order following Vancouver style.
Figures require numeric labels and concise explanatory captions.
Abstract should succinctly describe objective, method, results, and conclusion.
Section flow should follow Abstract, Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, References.
Use bracketed numeric references in order of citation.
Figures should be sequentially numbered with descriptive captions and in-text references.
